title: Exact Entanglement-Depth Speed Frontier for Complete Quantum Charging
authors: Wenlong Sun, Gang Lu, Yuanfeng Jin
abstract: Complete quantum charging provides a sharp setting in which to ask how much multipartite entanglement is forced by speed itself. For a closed \(N\)-qubit battery evolving from \(\ket{\downarrow}^{\otimes N}\) to \(\ket{\uparrow}^{\otimes N}\) under a time-independent Hamiltonian, we exactly solve the pure-state depth-constrained speed problem. If the realized trajectory has entanglement depth at most \(k\), then the largest possible QSL-normalized rate \(η=τ_{\rm QSL}/T\) is \(η_{\max}(k)=\lceil N/k\rceil^{-1/2}\). Conversely, an observed rate \(η\) certifies trajectory entanglement depth at least \(\bigl\lceil N/\lfloor η^{-2}\rfloor\bigr\rceil\). The mechanism is block orthogonalization: under a fixed product partition, complete charging forces all blocks to orthogonalize simultaneously, and the quantum speed limit converts this counting constraint into the speed bound. Balanced cluster-flip evolutions saturate the bound, establishing an exact integer staircase frontier. Thus fast complete charging cannot be explained by many small independently charging blocks; in particular, crossing the threshold \(η>1/\sqrt2\) certifies, for \(N>1\), the generation of genuine \(N\)-partite entanglement.
summary: This paper proves that the speed of charging a quantum battery is fundamentally limited by the amount of multipartite entanglement present in the system. It establishes an exact 'staircase' relationship where faster charging rates directly certify higher levels of entanglement depth, including genuine N-partite entanglement.
main_problem: Determining the fundamental relationship between the speed of a complete quantum charging process and the amount of multipartite entanglement depth required to achieve that speed.
main_result: The authors establish an exact integer staircase frontier relating the QSL-normalized rate to entanglement depth, proving that crossing the threshold eta > 1/sqrt(2) certifies genuine N-partite entanglement.
method: The study uses Quantum Speed Limit (QSL) geometry, specifically the Mandelstam-Tamm bound, combined with Minkowski's integral inequality and a Baire category argument to relate path speed to block orthogonalization.
model_or_system: A closed N-qubit quantum battery undergoing unitary evolution from an all-down state to an all-up state under a time-independent Hamiltonian.
key_observables: QSL-normalized rate (eta), entanglement depth (Ent[U]), and the number of independent blocks (m).
important_parameters: N-qubit system size, entanglement depth constraint (k), and the threshold eta = 1/sqrt(2) for N-partite entanglement.
main_assumptions: The results apply to finite-dimensional pure-state complete charging under a closed, time-independent Hamiltonian with exact endpoint conditions.
figures_summary: Figure 1 displays the exact integer staircase frontier for N=20, plotting the charging rate against the lower bound on entanglement depth.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of quantum charging speed, defines the QSL-normalized rate, provides the mathematical derivation of the speed-depth frontier using block orthogonalization, constructs a saturated model to prove sharpness, and concludes with entanglement certification thresholds.
why_interesting: It provides a model-independent, orbit-level diagnostic for quantifying many-body entanglement resources based solely on the observed speed of a dynamical process.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
